code.COMMENT: Unique identifier which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

java_class.COMMENT: The java class which represents the entity within the core application.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier (within a scope) which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name (within a scope) which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

shared.COMMENT:
true: Can be used by multiple entitites.
false: Only allowed to be used by a single entity

sql_text.COMMENT: This can be used if the list of values is not static and needs to be derived from live data.  The SQL should return exactly two columns: name, value.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

description.COMMENT: A longer description of the field which is displayed to the user as context sensitive help.

environment_specific.COMMENT: ONLY USED FOR THE SCOPE ENTITY TYPE - indicates that this field is environment specific and should not be exported/imported from one environment to another.

disabled.COMMENT: Similar to a logical delete: used to "remove" a field without physically deleting it.  This is needed because the extension key/value pair rows have a FK to this table.  A disabled field will not be displayed on any views and will not be validated.

required.COMMENT: The field must be provided by the user of the web site and included in any batch loads.

regex.COMMENT: Regular expression that is used to validate the fields data.  For example "^[a-z]*$" would be used to enforce that only lower case alpha characters are provided.

regex_display.COMMENT: A text description of the regular expression which can be displayed to the user.  Example: Alpha-numeric, numeric, etc.

min_length.COMMENT: Minimum number of characters allowed for this field.

max_length.COMMENT: Maximum number of characters allowed for this field.  The extension records allow for 30,000 characters.

display_order.COMMENT: Can be used to control the order the fields are displayed when no view is defined.  This is not normally used as a view is normally defined to control the fields displayed.

value.COMMENT: Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.
Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier (within a scope) which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

temporary.COMMENT: When the customer has an there own system to manage students, the temporary flag indicates that the student was added in our system to facilitate testing.

local_code.COMMENT: The customer provided identifier for this student.  This field might be used by the customer to "help" identify an student, however it is not guaranteed to be unique.

middle_name.COMMENT: Students middle name or middle initial.

value.COMMENT: Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.
Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.
Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier (within a scope) which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name (within a scope) which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

exclusive_student.COMMENT: If this flag is true, students can only be assigned to this group if they do not already belong to a group.

description.COMMENT: Detail description of the group type which can be used for context-sensitive help.

name.COMMENT: Unique name (within an organization) which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

description.COMMENT: Detailed description of the group which can be displayed to the user as context sensitive help.

grade.COMMENT: The grade level of the students in this group.

name.COMMENT: Unique name (within an option list) which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

value.COMMENT: Unique value (within an option list) which will be stored to the database.

display_order.COMMENT: Controls the order in which the values for an option list are displayed to the user.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier (within a scope) which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name (within a scope) which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

description.COMMENT: Detail description of the query that can be displayed to the user.

keywords.COMMENT: Comma separated list of key words that can be used by a developer to search for a query to use.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier that can be used code to lookup a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique (within scope) name that is displayed to the user.

category.COMMENT: What category is this view type (see enum for possible values)

defaultView.COMMENT: Is this the default view for the specified "entity_type_id"?  There should be only one default view per entity type.

name.COMMENT: Optional name that can be provided to override the default (hard coded) view name displayed on a web page.

collapsible.COMMENT: Can the area on the screen displaying this view definition be collapsed.

collapsed_by_default.COMMENT: Should the area on the screen displaying this view definition be collapsed by default.

column1_width.COMMENT: The width of the first column.

column1_label_width.COMMENT: The width of the labels in the first column.

column2_width.COMMENT: The width of the second column.

column2_label_width.COMMENT: The width of the labels in the second column.

column3_width.COMMENT: The width of the third column.

column3_label_width.COMMENT: The width of the labels in the third column.

read_only.COMMENT: If "true" the field will be displayed as static text and the user will be unable to edit the field.

override_name.COMMENT: Can be used to display a different field label than the name defined on the entity.

input_type.COMMENT: For fields with option lists, determines which control should be displayed. (see enum for possible values)

label_position.COMMENT: How should the label for this field be positioned (see enum for possible values)

display_width.COMMENT: What should the CSS width for this input. (ex: 60px, 20em)

column_number.COMMENT: Which column should this field be displayed.

display_order.COMMENT: Controls the order of the fields displayed on the page.  Fields with lower numbers are displayed first.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier (within a scope) which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name (within a scope) which is displayed to the user.  This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

allow_paper.COMMENT: Is this a test that can be delivered on paper?

allow_online.COMMENT: Is this a test that can be delivered online?

allow_alternate.COMMENT: Is this a test that is an alternate assessment?

type.COMMENT:
paper - printed document
online - computer based test
alternative - instructor evaluation

status.COMMENT:
assign - not yet started
testing - testing in progress
attempt - test complete and valid
invalid_attempt - test complete with problems

preid_code.COMMENT: Unique barcode number which is printed on test booklets and used for test identification after scanning.

value.COMMENT: Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.

code.COMMENT: Unique (within scope) code that can be used by import, exports, etc.

name.COMMENT: Unique (within scope) name that is displayed to the user.

display_order.COMMENT: Used to force the order (ascending) in which the contact types are displayed on the website.

name.COMMENT: Full name of the person (first, middle, last)

phone.COMMENT: Phone number ... 20 digits to accomodate international numbers

fax.COMMENT: Fax number ... 20 digits to accomodate international numbers

username.COMMENT: The username (namespaced by the scope path) corresponds to the authenticated username used to access the application.

active_begin_date.COMMENT: If the current date is before the active_begin_date, the user is unauthorized to access the application.

active_end_date.COMMENT: If the current date is after the active_end_date, the user is unauthorized to access the application.

disable_date.COMMENT: If this date is non-null, the user is unauthorized to access the application.

disable_reason.COMMENT: A descriptive field which allows the admin to indicate why the user was disabled.

delete_date.COMMENT: If this date is non-null, the user is unauthorized to access the application.

reset_token_1.COMMENT: The unique token sent to the user in emails to allow the user to reset their password (last 5 are saved)

reset_token_2.COMMENT: The unique token sent to the user in emails to allow the user to reset their password (last 5 are saved)

reset_token_3.COMMENT: The unique token sent to the user in emails to allow the user to reset their password (last 5 are saved)

reset_token_4.COMMENT: The unique token sent to the user in emails to allow the user to reset their password (last 5 are saved)

reset_token_5.COMMENT: The unique token sent to the user in emails to allow the user to reset their password (last 5 are saved)

selected_scope_id.COMMENT: Scope that should be the default selected global scope for this user at login.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name which is displayed to the user. This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

category.COMMENT: Display only ... used to group related roles on edit pages

short_name.COMMENT: A short name that can be used on the site when space is limited.  If not provided name will be displayed.

description.COMMENT: A longer description of the role which is displayed to the user as context sensitive help.

display_order.COMMENT: Can be used to control the order the features are displayed on our web pages.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: nique name which is displayed to the user. This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

description.COMMENT: A longer description of the field which is displayed to the user as context sensitive help.

display_order.COMMENT: Can be used to control the order the permissions are displayed on our web pages.

value.COMMENT: Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.

code.COMMENT: Unique identifier which can be used by applications as a key to retrieve a specific row.

name.COMMENT: Unique name which is displayed to the user. This descriptive name can change and should not be be used as a key.

description.COMMENT: A longer description of the field which is displayed to the user as context sensitive help.

display_order.COMMENT: Can be used to control the order the features are displayed on our web pages.

disabled.COMMENT: Forces a feature to be made inactive and disables all associated permissions.

begin_date.COMMENT: The feature is considered inactive if the current date is before the begin_date.

end_date.COMMENT: The feature is considered inactive if the current date is after the end_date.

code.COMMENT: Unique (within scope) identifier that can be used code to lookup a specific row.  This code should match the keys to the message bundles.

text.COMMENT: The text that is displayed to the user.
The text (HTML is supported) to be displayed to the user.

column_number.COMMENT: Which column should this text be displayed.

display_order.COMMENT: The order in which this text should be displayed ... used in conjunction with view_def_field.display_order.

count.COMMENT: The number of devices with this identical configuration.

processor.COMMENT: Type of processor (possible values in option_list)

storage.COMMENT: Storage (hard disk, etc) space (possible values in option_list)

flash_version.COMMENT: Version of the flash player (possible values in option_list)

java_version.COMMENT: Java version installed (possible values in option_list)

browser.COMMENT: Primary browser used (possible values in option_list)

screen_resolution.COMMENT: Size of the display (possible values in option_list)

value.COMMENT: Value for the "key" referenced by the entity_field_id.

code.COMMENT: Unique code that can be used by import, exports, etc.

name.COMMENT: Unique name that is displayed to the user.

status.COMMENT:
Indicates the state of the processing of the file.
pending:Processing has not yet started.
processing:Currently processing the file.
complete:Processing completed succesfully.
errors:Processing completed with errors.

path.COMMENT: The file system path used to access the file.

filename.COMMENT: This is the real (generated) filename used to reference the file within the file system.

display_filename.COMMENT: The name of the file that will be displayed to the user (ex: original uploaded filename).

status_message.COMMENT: A detailed description (ex: 7 of the 99 rows had errors) of the status of the file.

mode.COMMENT:
How should the data in this file be processed?
overwrite: Perform matching... overwrite existing data and insert new data.
append: Simply insert (no updates) the records in the file.
replace: Delete all of the data for this org and then insert the records in this file.

request_date.COMMENT: The date the user requested processing of the file.

description.COMMENT: An option user provided description of the file.

total_record_count.COMMENT: Count of the number of records contained in the file ... provided by the first step in the batch job.

kilobytes.COMMENT: The size of the file in kilobytes.

batch_job_execution_id.COMMENT: The ID of the Spring Batch job execution table ... used to get detailed information about the processing of the file.

record_number.COMMENT: The record number (line number for CSV, entity number for XML, etc) that has the error.  A null value indicates that the error is global and not specific to any record.

error_code.COMMENT: Unique code that indicates the specific error being reported.

message.COMMENT: The detailed discription of the error to be displayed to the user.
